danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprites inhabiting each island stage
danger_signals.meaning: Hostile entities controlled by the Evil Witch Doctor; visual markers of danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinct enemy sprite at stage beginning or relocated to new areas after defeat
danger_signals.meaning: Escalated danger; island's primary threat that must be overcome to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental hazards with distinct visual properties
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death or movement-altering obstacles; danger zones that require specific dinosaur allies or careful navigation

danger_signals.visual_form: Water-filled level environments with distinct visual palette
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental danger requiring specialized dinosaur ally (elasmosaurus) to survive

danger_signals.visual_form: Camera orientation shift to vertical perspective
danger_signals.meaning: Heightened difficulty or danger requiring avoidance of falling and environmental threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Rideable dinosaur sprite alongside Master Higgins
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced survivability; temporary protection and special abilities active

safety_signals.visual_form: Hammer item in inventory or visual representation on HUD
safety_signals.meaning: Increased offensive capability; stored hammers indicate readiness for combat

safety_signals.visual_form: Special visual indicator (distinct noise cue for locating hidden eggs)
safety_signals.meaning: Access to bonus zones, item rooms, or shortcuts; progression opportunities and resource gathering
Player control over movement; strategic planning capability for stage selection
Increased mobility and speed; temporary safety through movement advantage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dinosaur allies in different colors: blue camptosaurus, red camptosaurus, pteranodon, elasmosaurus
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Skateboard and hammer equipment; presence on map screen

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monster minions and bosses inhabiting each of eight islands; boss relocates visually to new areas upon defeat
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Control over eight perilous islands; presence of lava, ice, and underwater hazards
Quest objective; awaits rescue at end of progression through islands

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Playing card suits: heart (blue camptosaurus), spade (red camptosaurus), clover (pteranodon), diamond (elasmosaurus)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Summoned as allies; each has distinct visual appearance and movement characteristics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Found within hidden eggs as card rewards; positioned to counter specific island hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite visible at beginning of specific stage; standard enemy density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters player immediately; if defeated, boss relocates to another area requiring additional stage completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss no longer present in original location; different stage area contains relocated boss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must clear additional stage to re-engage boss; increases stage path complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map screen displays completed and uncompleted islands; visual indicators of progress across eight islands
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can backtrack within stages up to certain point; strategic choice of next island available

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dinosaur sprite appears alongside Master Higgins; specific dinosaur type visible (blue, red, pteranodon, elasmosaurus)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Special abilities active: tail attacks, fire breathing, flight, underwater speed boost depending on dinosaur type

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spinning egg selector appears with ten egg choices; visual reward mechanism
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player selects egg to receive point rewards; progress toward next island recorded on map

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lava terrain visible with distinct visual properties distinct from standard ground
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Red camptosaurus can breathe fire and traverse lava temporarily; other dinosaurs lost if submerged; hazard damage on contact

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ice terrain with slippery visual appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Blue camptosaurus can walk on ice without sliding; standard dinosaurs and Higgins slide uncontrollably; movement challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Water-filled level environment with aquatic visual palette and distinct sprite behavior
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elasmosaurus required for survival and speed boost; other dinosaurs lost immediately upon water contact

environmental_storytelling.example: Each island features lava, ice, or water hazards that directly correspond to available dinosaur allies (fire camptosaurus for lava, ice-walking camptosaurus for ice, elasmosaurus for water). Hazard placement tells story of island environment and required preparation.

Master Higgins must traverse eight islands controlled by monster minions to reach kidnapped girlfriend Tina. Island numbers and boss relocations create visual sense of persistent opposition and escalating difficulty toward goal.

Four dinosaur types with distinct colors and abilities represent different island solutions. Hidden within eggs as card suits, their availability and player choice creates narrative of selective alliance building.
When defeated, boss visually moves to new location, creating sense of cunning opposition and world reaction to player actions. Requires player to pursue and re-engage, extending narrative tension.
Map screen visually displays eight islands and progression through them. Uncompleted islands appear distinct from completed ones, creating visual sense of expanding conquered territory.
Skateboard and stone hammers are visible equipment that Master Higgins carries and deploys. Inventory system and visual presence of tools signal preparation and resource management.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traverses stages left-to-right (standard platformer flow); can backtrack within stage limits
Rideable; movement speed increases; special abilities activate (fire breath, flight, tail attack, aquatic speed)
Can strike objects and enemies with increased damage; no firepower upgrade available (hammers accumulate in inventory instead)
Increased movement speed; skateboard cannot carry over to next stage after completion
Positioned at beginning of specific stage; engages player immediately upon stage entry
Visually relocates to different stage area; player must complete additional stage to re-engage; indicates world reaction to player threat
Populate respective islands under Witch Doctor control; serve as primary stage hazards distinct from environmental obstacles
Walks without sliding; maintains control on slippery surfaces; visual indicator of terrain compatibility
Breathes fire; traverses lava safely for limited duration; exhibits fire immunity behavior
Flies over obstacles; drops stones as attack; bypasses ground-based hazards through aerial movement
Only dinosaur that survives underwater; increases swimming speed; other dinosaurs lost on water contact (visual despawn)
Remain invisible to player; emit distinct audio cue when Higgins strikes their location
Grant access to bonus zones, item rooms, or stage shortcuts; create branching path progression

notes: Adventure Island II communicates world-state primarily through visual island environment variation (lava, ice, water), dinosaur ally diversity tied to environmental hazards, and boss relocation mechanic that signals world reaction to player progress. The game uses color-coded dinosaurs (blue, red, etc.) with associated playing card suits as faction indicators for the allied forces. Danger is communicated through hazard visibility (lava pools, ice slicks, underwater depth) and enemy sprite placement. Safety is signaled through dinosaur ally presence and equipment inventory. The map screen provides strategic clarity of progression across eight islands. The rescue narrative of Master Higgins pursuing kidnapped Tina creates overarching world-state context that justifies the eight-island progression structure.
sources_used: wikipedia - Adventure Island II article (plot, gameplay mechanics, dinosaur abilities, stage types, inventory system)
